_meta.description: Seed catalog: the game gear type → recommended VST3/AU plugins. Free plugins only. Used by /vst/suggest endpoint and the per-piece VST panel hint.
_meta.notes: v7: bundled clones only; adds Electar Model M and Electric Hawaiian aliases to EpicallCentura. Every gear resolves to its Rig Builder bundled VST (or NAM if none). No external/third-party plugins.

Pedal_AcousticEmulator.name: AcousticSimulator
Pedal_AcousticEmulator.notes: Bundled blue-button acoustic simulator based on the local schematic, with TL072/TL061 stages, 2N4339/J201 FET voice branch, 1N4148/green LED diode ladder, and real Gain, Top, Body, Volume controls. Rocksmith Tone maps to Top, Body to Body, Mid to Gain, and Volume is pinned.

Pedal_Chorus.name: Chorus
Pedal_Chorus.notes: Bundled Boss CE-2 style MN3007/MN3101 BBD chorus based on the local schematic. Real pedal controls: Rate and Depth; Rocksmith Mix is intentionally not exposed because the CE-2 has a fixed dry/wet mixer.

Pedal_Chorus20.name: Chorus20
Pedal_Chorus20.notes: Bundled Deja Vibe/Uni-Vibe style optical chorus based on the local ElectroVibe/UniVibe schematics. Real controls: Intensity, Speed1, Speed2, Speed Select, Volume, Mode; Rocksmith pins Mode to Chorus.

Pedal_StereoChorus.name: AnalogChorus
Pedal_StereoChorus.notes: Bundled MXR-style stereo analog BBD chorus with real Bass, Treble, Intensity, Width, and Speed controls. Mono input is expanded to true L/R modulation; stereo input is preserved. Rocksmith Rate/Depth/Mix map to Speed/Width/Intensity.

Pedal_DigitalChorus.name: DigitalChorus
Pedal_DigitalChorus.notes: Bundled Boss CE-5 style digital chorus based on the local schematic. Real controls: E.Level, Rate, Depth, LoFilter, HiFilter; mono input is expanded to true L/R modulation and Rocksmith Mix maps to E.Level.

Pedal_VintageChorus.name: AnalogChorus
Pedal_VintageChorus.notes: Bundled warm MXR-style analog stereo BBD chorus based on the local analog chorus schematic. Real controls: Bass, Treble, Intensity, Width, Speed; mono input is expanded to true L/R modulation and Rocksmith Rate/Depth/Mix map to Speed/Width/Intensity.

Bass_Pedal_BassChorus.name: BassChorus
Bass_Pedal_BassChorus.notes: Bundled Boss CEB-3 style model using the ES56028 short digital delay core from the local schematic, with NJM022/M5223 analog conditioning and the real Rate, Depth, Low Filter, and E.Level controls. Mono input is expanded to true L/R modulation; Rocksmith LoFilter maps to Low Filter and Mix maps to E.Level.

Pedal_Flanger.name: ClassicFlanger
Pedal_Flanger.notes: Bundled Boss BF-2 style component-guided BBD flanger. Real controls: Manual, Depth, Rate, Res; Rocksmith Rate/Depth map directly and Mix maps to Res because the hardware has fixed wet/dry blend.

Pedal_80sFlanger.name: EightiesFlanger
Pedal_80sFlanger.notes: Bundled MXR M117 Flanger Original/SAD1024 style model based on the local 80s flanger schematic. Real controls: Manual, Width, Speed, Regen; Rocksmith Rate maps to Speed, Depth to Width, and Mix to Regen.

Pedal_ClassicFlanger.name: ClassicFlanger
Pedal_ClassicFlanger.notes: Bundled Boss BF-2/MN3207 style component-guided BBD flanger based on the local classic schematic. Real controls: Manual, Depth, Rate, Res; Rocksmith Mix maps to Res.

Pedal_ModernFlanger.name: FM108
Pedal_ModernFlanger.notes: Bundled Moog MF-108M Cluster Flux style component-guided BBD flanger based on the local modern flange schematic. Real controls: Time, Range, Feedback, Drive, Output Level, Mix, LFO Shape, LFO Rate, LFO Amount; Rocksmith Rate/Depth/Regen/Mix map to LFO Rate/LFO Amount/Feedback/Mix and Range is pinned to FLANGE.

Pedal_VintageFlanger.name: VintageFlanger
Pedal_VintageFlanger.notes: Bundled Deluxe Electric Mistress style component-guided BBD flanger based on the local vintage schematic. Real controls: Rate, Range, Color, Matrix; Rocksmith Depth maps to Range and Mix maps to Color.

Bass_Pedal_BassFlanger.name: BassFlanger
Bass_Pedal_BassFlanger.notes: Bundled Boss BF-2B style component-guided bass flanger using M5218P/TL022 conditioning and MN3102/MN3204 BBD delay. Real controls: Manual, Depth, Rate, Res; Rocksmith Filter/Mix translate to the real resonance path.

Pedal_Phaser.name: Phaser363
Pedal_Phaser.notes: Bundled MXR Phase 90 style four-stage phaser based on the local Phaser 363 schematic, using the game's single Rate knob.

Pedal_ShaverPhaser.name: ShaverPhaser
Pedal_ShaverPhaser.notes: Bundled ET-25B style phaser based on the local shaver phaser schematic, using the game's Rate and Depth knobs with fixed internal mix.

Pedal_PlanePhase.name: PlanePhase
Pedal_PlanePhase.notes: Bundled AP-7 style eight-stage phaser based on the local Plane Phase schematic, using the game's Rate, Depth, and Mix knobs.

Bass_Pedal_BassPhase.name: BassPhase
Bass_Pedal_BassPhase.notes: Bundled analog bass phaser — 4 swept all-pass stages with regeneration, dry/wet notch blend, and a wet-path high-pass so bass lows stay solid. Maps RS Rate/Depth/Mix/Filter.

Pedal_Tremolo.name: Tremolo
Pedal_Tremolo.notes: Bundled Colorsound Tremolo/Tremola style transistor tremolo based on the local schematic, using the game's Speed and Mix knobs.

Pedal_AmpTrem.name: AmpTrem
Pedal_AmpTrem.notes: Bundled Demeter Tremulator-style optical amp tremolo based on the local schematic, using the game's Speed and Depth knobs.

Pedal_MultiTrem.name: MultiTrem
Pedal_MultiTrem.notes: Bundled Boss TR-2 style multi-wave tremolo based on the local schematic, using the game's Speed, Mix, and Waveform knobs.

Pedal_TremOle.name: TremOle
Pedal_TremOle.notes: Bundled envelope-aware stereo tremolo using the game's Sens, Attack, Release, and Mix knobs.

Pedal_AmpVibe.name: AmpVibe
Pedal_AmpVibe.notes: Bundled Uni-Vibe style four-stage phase modulation based on the local schematic, using the game's Speed and Mix knobs.

Pedal_MultiVibe.name: MultiVibe
Pedal_MultiVibe.notes: Bundled Boss VB-2 style BBD vibrato based on the local schematic, using the game's Speed, Mix and Waveform knobs.

Pedal_AutoVibe.name: AutoVibe
Pedal_AutoVibe.notes: Bundled envelope-controlled vibrato/vibe using the game's Sens, Attack, Release, and Mix knobs.

Pedal_BassEmulator.name: BassEmulator
Pedal_BassEmulator.notes: Bundled guitar-to-bass octave emulator using the game's Body and Tone knobs.

Pedal_MarshallSupervibe.name: MarshallSupervibe
Pedal_MarshallSupervibe.notes: Bundled Marshall SV-1/Supervibe style MN3007 BBD vibe based on the local schematic, using the game's Rate, Depth, Mix and Wave knobs.

Pedal_BakedRotatoe.name: BakedRotatoe
Pedal_BakedRotatoe.notes: Bundled Leslie/RT-20 style rotary speaker based on the local LERA Leslie rotor adapter reference, using the game's Rate, Depth, Mix and Balance knobs.

Pedal_OmniMod.name: OmniMod
Pedal_OmniMod.notes: Bundled Uni-Vibe/Shin-ei style photocell phase modulation based on the local Omni Mod schematics, using the game's Rate, Depth, and Mix knobs.

Pedal_AnalogueDelay.name: AnalogDelay
Pedal_AnalogueDelay.notes: Bundled MF-104 style BBD delay using the game Time, Feedback and Mix controls

Pedal_ValveEcho.name: ValveEcho
Pedal_ValveEcho.notes: Bundled Binson Echorec style valve drum echo using the game Time, Feedback and Mix controls

Pedal_NPNDelay.name: NpnDelay
Pedal_NPNDelay.notes: Bundled DM-2 style BBD/NPN delay using the game Time, Feedback and Mix controls

Pedal_NoFiEcho.name: NoFiEcho
Pedal_NoFiEcho.notes: Bundled DE7-style stereo no-fi echo using the game Time, Feedback and Mix controls

Pedal_OilCanEcho.name: OilCanEcho
Pedal_OilCanEcho.notes: Bundled Tel-Ray / OK Pacemaker oil-can echo based on the local schematics, using the game's Time, Feedback, and Mix knobs.

Pedal_CosmicEcho.name: CosmicEcho
Pedal_CosmicEcho.notes: Bundled Synthrotek ECHO / PT2399-style cosmic delay based on the local schematic, using the game's Time, Feedback, and Mix knobs.

Pedal_ModDelay.name: ModDelay
Pedal_ModDelay.notes: Bundled DLL10/DML10-style modulated delay using the game's Time, Feedback, Mix, Rate, and Depth knobs.

Bass_Pedal_BassFilterDelay.name: BassFilterDelay
Bass_Pedal_BassFilterDelay.notes: Bundled Boss DM-2 analog (BBD) delay — warm repeats that darken cumulatively via a feedback-loop low-pass + gentle saturation. Maps RS Time/Feedback/Mix/Filter.

Bass_Pedal_BassFilterEcho.name: BassFilterEcho
Bass_Pedal_BassFilterEcho.notes: Bundled vintage tape echo — same engine as the Filter Delay but voiced like tape: darker, more saturated repeats with wow & flutter. Maps RS Time/Feedback/Mix/Filter.

Pedal_SpringReverb.name: SpringReverb
Pedal_SpringReverb.notes: Bundled Holy Grail-style spring tank reverb using the game's Time, Mix, and Depth knobs.

Pedal_DigitalVerb.name: DigitalVerb
Pedal_DigitalVerb.notes: Bundled Boss RV-2 style early digital reverb based on the local schematic, using the game's Time, Mix, Depth, and Tone knobs.

Pedal_PlateVerb.name: PlateVerb
Pedal_PlateVerb.notes: Bundled EMT 140-style plate reverb based on the local schematic, using the game's Time, Depth, Mix, and Voice knobs.

Pedal_TubeSpring.name: TubeSpring
Pedal_TubeSpring.notes: Bundled tube/spring tank reverb based on the local spring driver schematic, using the game's Mix and Depth knobs.

Pedal_Compression.name: DynamicsCompression
Pedal_Compression.notes: Bundled CA3080 Dyna Comp-style compressor with real Output and Sensitivity controls. Rocksmith Comp maps to Sensitivity; Output is pinned by the curated mapping.

Pedal_Compressor.name: DynamicsCompression
Pedal_Compressor.notes: Bundled CA3080 Dyna Comp-style compressor with real Output and Sensitivity controls. Rocksmith Comp maps to Sensitivity; Output is pinned by the curated mapping.

Bass_Pedal_MBComp.name: BassMultiComp
Bass_Pedal_MBComp.notes: Bundled EBS MultiComp 2-style bass compressor based on the local schematic, with real Comp, Sens, Gain and Mode controls. Rocksmith Compress maps to Comp; Filter maps to Sens; Gain and Mode are pinned.

Pedal_EQ5.name: EQ5
Pedal_EQ5.notes: Bundled 5-band graphic EQ, Mesa Boogie 5-band model, RS bands

Pedal_EQ8.name: EQ8
Pedal_EQ8.notes: Bundled 8-band graphic EQ clone of the game EQ8

Pedal_AmpEQ.name: Amp EQ
Pedal_AmpEQ.notes: Bundled Fender '59 Bassman 5F6-A passive FMV tone stack (Yeh & Smith DAFx-06 model). Bass/Mid/Treble interact with the real mid scoop; BassFreq/MidShift/TrebleFreq scale the tone-stack caps.

Bass_Pedal_BassEQ8.name: Bass EQ8
Bass_Pedal_BassEQ8.notes: Bundled 8-band bass graphic EQ, Boss GE-7B gyrator model, RS bands

Rack_StudioCompressor.name: Studio Comp
Rack_StudioCompressor.notes: Bundled dbx 160 model — true-RMS feed-forward VCA compressor (dbx 202 RMS detector + log VCA, OverEasy soft knee). Threshold/Ratio/Attack/Release map 1:1 to RS.

Rack_StudioGraphicEQ.name: Studio Graphic EQ
Rack_StudioGraphicEQ.notes: Bundled 5-band sweepable EQ, API-550 proportional-Q style

Pedal_NoiseGate.name: NoiseGate
Pedal_NoiseGate.notes: Bundled ET-45C/default-gate style gate using the game's Thresh and Rate knobs

Pedal_Limiter.name: Limiter
Pedal_Limiter.notes: Bundled Boss LM-2-style VCA limiter with real Level, Tone, Release and Threshold controls. Rocksmith Limit maps to Threshold; Rate maps inverted to Release.

Pedal_USWah.name: USWah
Pedal_USWah.notes: Bundled Dunlop Cry Baby GCB-95 style wah based on the local US Wah schematic, using the game's Auto, Pedal, Sens, and Speed knobs.

Pedal_UKWah.name: UKWah
Pedal_UKWah.notes: Bundled Vox V847 / Clyde McCoy style wah based on the local UK Wah schematic, using the game's Auto, Pedal, Sens, and Speed knobs.

Bass_Pedal_BassWah.name: BassWah
Bass_Pedal_BassWah.notes: Bundled Cry Baby Bass Wah (105Q) model — resonant band-pass swept across 100 Hz-1.6 kHz by Pedal position, the input envelope (Sens) and an LFO (Speed) when Auto is on, with a little dry blend to keep bass body. Maps RS Auto/Pedal/Sens/Speed.

Pedal_ModernWah.name: ModernWah
Pedal_ModernWah.notes: Bundled Morley Bad Horsie / optical contour wah based on the local Modern Wah schematic, using the game's Auto, Pedal, Sens, and Speed knobs.

Pedal_LoFiFilter.name: LoFiFilter
Pedal_LoFiFilter.notes: Bundled Lofinator-style lo-fi filter based on the local schematic. The plugin exposes the real Drive, Level, Lo and Hi controls; Rocksmith FilterType/Mix are translated in the mapping table.

Pedal_BobFilter.name: BobFilter
Pedal_BobFilter.notes: Bundled Moog/MuRF-style analog filter based on the local MF-105 schematic. The plugin exposes Drive, Output, Pattern, Rate, Envelope, Mix and Mode; Rocksmith Sens/Attack/Release/Mix/Filter are translated in the mapping table.

Pedal_AutoFilter.name: AutoFilter
Pedal_AutoFilter.notes: Bundled Mu-Tron III style envelope filter based on the local Auto Filter schematic. The plugin exposes Gain, Peak, Mode, Range and Direction; Rocksmith FilterType/Res/Sens/Attack/Release are translated in the mapping table.

Pedal_AutoFilter.name: AutoSweep
Pedal_AutoFilter.notes: Bundled envelope filter / auto-wah (ships in vst/Qtrix.vst3). Real envelope follower -> biquad sweep, no separate install.

Bass_Pedal_BassAutoFilter.name: AutoSweep
Bass_Pedal_BassAutoFilter.notes: Bundled Q-Tron-style envelope filter kept for the game Bass Auto Filter/Bass Auto Tone, with bass-range sweep calibration; guitar Auto Tone uses the Mu-Tron III clone.

Pedal_GermaniumDrive.name: GermaniumDrive
Pedal_GermaniumDrive.notes: Bundled Skywave/Broadcast-style germanium preamp drive based on the local schematic. Real controls: Gain, LowCut, Level, GainMode, Voltage; RS Tone maps to LowCut.

Pedal_CustomDrive.name: CustomDrive
Pedal_CustomDrive.notes: Bundled MOSFET/diode custom overdrive based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Drive, Tone, Voice, Volume; RS Gain maps to Drive.

Pedal_LineDrive.name: LineDrive
Pedal_LineDrive.notes: Bundled Boss OS-2 style drive/distortion blend based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Drive, Tone, Color, Level; RS Gain maps to Drive.

Pedal_MarshallGuvnorPlus.name: MarshallGuvnorPlus
Pedal_MarshallGuvnorPlus.notes: Bundled Marshall GV-2 Guv'nor Plus style drive based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Gain, Bass, Mid, Treble, Deep, Volume.

Pedal_SuperDrive.name: SuperDrive
Pedal_SuperDrive.notes: Bundled Boss SD-1 style asymmetric feedback overdrive based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Drive, Tone, Level; RS Gain maps to Drive.

Pedal_RangeBooster.name: RangeBooster
Pedal_RangeBooster.notes: Bundled Rangemaster-style treble booster based on the local OC44 Range Booster schematic, using the game's Boost knob.

Pedal_Distortion.name: StandardDistortion
Pedal_Distortion.notes: Bundled Boss DS-1 style standard distortion based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Dist, Tone, Level; RS Gain maps to Dist.

Pedal_VintageDistortion.name: VintageDistortion
Pedal_VintageDistortion.notes: Bundled DOD 250 style overdrive/preamp based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Gain, Volume; RS Tone is not mapped because the pedal has no tone control.

Pedal_MetalDistortion.name: AlloyDistortion
Pedal_MetalDistortion.notes: Bundled Boss HM-2 style heavy metal distortion based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Dist, ColorLow, ColorHigh, Level; RS Gain maps to Dist.

Bass_Pedal_BassDistortion.name: BassDistortion
Bass_Pedal_BassDistortion.notes: Bundled Pro Co RAT model — LM308 high-gain + hard 1N4148 clip + passive RAT Filter. Real controls are Distortion/Filter/Volume; RS Gain maps to Distortion, RS Filter/Tone map through the inverted RAT Filter, and Volume is pinned by the curated mapping.

Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive.name: BassOverdrive
Bass_Pedal_BassOverdrive.notes: Bundled Darkglass Microtubes B3K model — J201/TL072 front end, CD4049UBE CMOS clipping and clean blend. Real controls are Blend/Drive/Level plus Attack/Grunt switches; RS Tone/Filter drive the two quantized switches and Level is pinned by the curated mapping.

Pedal_FuzzWasHe.name: BZ-1
Pedal_FuzzWasHe.notes: Bundled Boss FZ-3-style silicon fuzz based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Fuzz, Tone, Volume.

Pedal_CaptFuzzle.name: Buzz-Tone
Pedal_CaptFuzzle.notes: Bundled two-knob low-headroom germanium fuzz inspired by a vintage fuzz-tone circuit.

Pedal_BuzzOne.name: Super-Buzz
Pedal_BuzzOne.notes: Bundled Univox Super-Fuzz-style octave fuzz based on the local schematic. Real controls: Expander, Tone SW, Balance; RS Gain maps to Expander.

Pedal_BuzzToo.name: Big Buzz
Pedal_BuzzToo.notes: Bundled V1 Big Muff-style fuzz based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Sustain, Tone, Volume; RS Gain maps to Sustain.

Bass_Pedal_BassFuzz.name: BassFuzz
Bass_Pedal_BassFuzz.notes: Bundled EHX Bass Big Muff Pi model — BC547 gain stages, dual 1N4148 clipping cells, Big Muff tone stack and Bass/Dry switch. Real controls are Sustain/Tone/Volume plus Bass/Dry; RS Filter drives the quantized switch and Volume is pinned by the curated mapping.

Pedal_OctaveUp.name: OctaveUp
Pedal_OctaveUp.notes: Bundled clean +12 octave-up inspired by Foxrox Octron and EQD Tentacle two-path octave references, using the game's Tone and Mix knobs.

Pedal_Octavius.name: Octavius
Pedal_Octavius.notes: Bundled Boss OC-2 style octave-down pedal based on the local Octavius schematic, using the game's Tone and Mix knobs.

Pedal_MultiPitch.name: MultiPitch
Pedal_MultiPitch.notes: Bundled MF-102 style ring mod pitch-sideband pedal based on the local multipitch schematic, using the game's Pitch1, Tone and Mix knobs.

Bass_Pedal_BassSubOctave.name: BassSubOctave
Bass_Pedal_BassSubOctave.notes: Bundled Boss OC-2 model — monophonic analog octaver: comparator + flip-flop frequency divider (one octave down), envelope-tracked, with a Tone low-pass and dry/sub Mix. Maps RS Mix/Tone.

Pedal_BitCruncher.name: BitCruncher
Pedal_BitCruncher.notes: Bundled Beverly BitCrusher/ADC0804-style lo-fi converter using the game's Attack, FilterType, Mix, Release, and Sens knobs.

Pedal_RingMod.name: RingMod
Pedal_RingMod.notes: Bundled Maestro/Oberheim RM-1A style ring modulator using the game's Depth, Waveform, Sensitivity and Attack knobs.

Pedal_Swole.name: Swole
Pedal_Swole.notes: Bundled Lazy Sprocket / Boss SG-1-style slow-gear circuit based on the local schematic. Real controls are Sensitivity and Attack; Rocksmith Smash maps to Sensitivity and Rate maps inverted to Attack.

Pedal_ShredZone.name: ShredZone
Pedal_ShredZone.notes: Bundled Boss MT-2 style high-gain distortion based on the local schematic. Real panel controls: Dist, Low, High, Middle, MiddleFreq, Level.

Pedal_Enbiggenator.name: Enbiggenator
Pedal_Enbiggenator.notes: Bundled the game-style modulated thickener/widener using the game's Rate, Depth, and Mix knobs.

Rack_StereoPhaser.name: StereoPhaser
Rack_StereoPhaser.notes: Bundled Stereo phaser. Maps RS Rate/Depth/Mix.

Rack_StudioWahFilter.name: StudioWahFilter
Rack_StudioWahFilter.notes: Bundled Auto wah filter. Maps RS Sens/Attack/Release/Pedal/Auto.

Rack_SynthFilter.name: SynthFilter
Rack_SynthFilter.notes: Bundled Envelope filterbank. Maps RS Sens/Attack/Release/FilterType/Mix.

Rack_RotaVibe.name: RotaVibe
Rack_RotaVibe.notes: Bundled Rotary speaker. Maps RS Rate/Depth/Mix/Balance.

Rack_StereoTubeTrem.name: StereoTubeTrem
Rack_StereoTubeTrem.notes: Bundled Tube tremolo. Maps RS Speed/Mix/Waveform.

Rack_StudioPitch.name: StudioPitch
Rack_StudioPitch.notes: Bundled Pitch shifter. Maps RS Pitch/Tone/Mix/Pan.

Bass_Pedal_BassEnbig.name: BassEnbig
Bass_Pedal_BassEnbig.notes: Bundled 'Enbiggenator' — a fictional RS pedal modeled as an LFO-modulated resonant band-pass (sci-fi vibe/wobble). Maps RS Rate/Depth/Mix/Filter.

Pedal_SendInTheClones.name: SendInTheClones
Pedal_SendInTheClones.notes: Bundled EHX Clone Theory style BBD chorus/vibrato based on the local layout. Real controls: Rate, Depth, ChVib, Flange; mono input is expanded to true L/R modulation and Rocksmith Clones/Depth/Mix map to Rate/Depth/ChVib.

Bass_Pedal_EdenWTDI.name: EdenWTDI
Bass_Pedal_EdenWTDI.notes: Bundled Eden WTDI model — preamp Gain drive + optical Compressor + 3-band active EQ (Bass/Mid/Treble ±15 dB) + Eden Enhance contour. The two red switches are LoShift (Bass Boost) and MidShift, modeled as toggles. Maps all 8 RS knobs.

Rack_StereoAnalogVibe.name: StereoAnalogVibe
Rack_StereoAnalogVibe.notes: Bundled Univibe vibrato. Maps RS Speed/Waveform/Mix.

Rack_StudioChamber.name: StudioChamber
Rack_StudioChamber.notes: Bundled Freeverb-style StudioChamber. Maps RS Time/Tone/Depth/Mix.

Rack_StudioChorus.name: StudioChorus
Rack_StudioChorus.notes: Bundled Boss RCE-10 Chorus Ensemble model — 12-bit digital chorus with NE572-style companding, pre/de-emphasis, R-2R quantization, effect EQ/level and stereo output spread. Maps all 7 RS knobs (Rate in Hz).

Rack_StudioDelay.name: StudioDelay
Rack_StudioDelay.notes: Bundled stereo delay with independent L/R times + Feedback + repeat Filter. Maps RS TimeL/TimeR(ms)/Feedback/Filter/Mix.

Rack_StudioFlanger.name: StudioFlanger
Rack_StudioFlanger.notes: Bundled stereo flanger — modulated short delay + Regen feedback + Tone low-pass. Maps RS Rate(Hz)/Depth/Regen/Tone/Mix.

Rack_StudioPlate.name: StudioPlate
Rack_StudioPlate.notes: Bundled Freeverb-style StudioPlate. Maps RS Time/Tone/Depth/Mix.

Rack_StudioVerb.name: StudioVerb
Rack_StudioVerb.notes: Bundled Freeverb-style StudioVerb. Maps RS Time/Tone/Depth/Mix.

Rack_TapeEcho.name: TapeEcho
Rack_TapeEcho.notes: Bundled Roland RE-201 Space Echo model — stereo tape echo, dark saturated repeats with wow & flutter + cross-feed. Maps RS Time(ms)/Feedback/Filter/Stereo/Mix.

Amp_CA100.name: DualRect
Amp_CA100.notes: Bundled Mesa-Boogie Dual Rectifier style amp (parody brand 'Silla'), full 3-channel panel modelled from the local Cali_100 schematic. The game's tones use Red/Modern/Bold: cascaded 12AX7 Recto preamp -> Recto tone stack -> 12AX7 LTP PI -> silicon/tube-selectable B+ nodes -> 4x6L6 power amp, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. Uses the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, Treble, and Pres knobs.

Amp_CA38.name: Silla Boogie Mark II
Amp_CA38.notes: Bundled Mesa/Boogie Mark IIB style amp (parody brand 'Silla'), full head panel (Volume 1[pull Bright], Treble[pull Shift], Bass, Middle, Master 1[pull Gain Boost], Lead Drive[pull Lead], Lead Master[pull Bright], Reverb, 100/60 RMS power) modelled component-by-component from the local Mark II schematic (boogie_mkii.pdf) + the panel photos. The CA_38 folder/schematic/photos are the Mark IIB. Scooped Boogie tone stack before the gain -> Rhythm/Lead 12AX7 paths -> Fender-style LTP PI -> stiff 6L6 B+ nodes -> 4x6L6 ~100W, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives Lead Drive; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack (Channel pinned Lead, reverb/pulls off via _static).

Amp_CA85.name: Silla Boogie Mark III
Amp_CA85.notes: Bundled Mesa/Boogie Mark III style amp (parody brand 'Silla'), full head panel (Volume[pull Bright], Treble, Bass, Middle, Master, Lead Drive, Lead Master, 5-band graphic EQ, Lead/EQ switches) modelled component-by-component from the local Mark III schematic. Scooped Boogie tone stack -> Rhythm/Lead 12AX7 paths -> 5-band GEQ -> Fender-style LTP PI -> Simul-Class B+ nodes -> 6L6 power, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives Lead Drive; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack (Channel pinned Lead + the GEQ 'V' via _static).

Amp_CS90.name: Ronald JC-90
Amp_CS90.notes: Bundled Roland JC-90 'Jazz Chorus' style SOLID-STATE amp (parody brand 'Ronald'), full front panel (Distortion, Volume, Hi-Treble/Treble/Middle/Bass, Reverb, Rate/Depth/Chorus stereo BBD chorus) modelled component-by-component from the local JC-90 schematic. the game's Gain knob drives the Distortion (clean at 0); Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Hi-Treble. Reverb/Chorus off for songs. Includes bypassable fallback Cab Sim.

Amp_EN30.name: BOX AC30
Amp_EN30.notes: Bundled Vox AC30 Top Boost / EL84 style amp based on the local EN30 schematics, using the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, Treble, Pres, and Bright knobs.

Amp_GB100.name: Raney AOR50
Amp_GB100.notes: Bundled Laney AOR 50 'Pro Tube Lead' (A50 Series II) style amp (parody brand 'Raney'), full 2-channel front panel (Channel One + AOR lead, each Preamp/Master with Pull-Bright, shared Bass/Pull-Deep, Middle/Pull-Boost, Treble, Presence) modelled component-by-component from the local AOR50 schematic. ECC83 cascade + real AOR diode clipper -> Marshall-style tone stack -> ECC83 LTP PI -> diode B+ nodes -> 2xEL34 power amp, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain knob drives the channel morph (Channel One clean -> AOR lead); Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence.

Amp_MarshallDSL100H.name: Marsten DSL100
Amp_MarshallDSL100H.notes: Bundled Marshall JCM2000 DSL100 style amp (parody brand 'Marsten'), full 2-channel front panel (Classic Clean/Crunch + Ultra OD1/OD2, Tone Shift, Resonance, Presence, dual Master, per-channel reverb, Low/High output) modelled component-by-component from the local DSL100 schematics, with ECC83 LTP PI, diode-rectified B+ nodes and 4xEL34 power amp. the game's Gain knob drives the channel morph (clean->crunch->ultra); Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence, Res->Resonance (Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_MarshallPlexi.name: Marsten Plexi
Amp_MarshallPlexi.notes: Bundled Marshall 1959 Super Lead 100W (Plexi/JMP) style amp (parody brand 'Marsten'), full 1959 front panel (Presence, Bass, Middle, Treble, Loudness I + Loudness II, 4 inputs) modelled component-by-component from the local 1959SLP schematic. NON-master amp: the Loudness pots ARE the gain, so the game's Gain knob drives Loudness I (clean->crunch->roar); Bass/Mid/Treble->Marshall tone stack (33K slope, 220pF treble cap), Pres->Presence. 4x EL34 power-amp grind plus host-controlled fallback Cab Sim.

Amp_BT45.name: Ganddi Superdrive 45
Amp_BT45.notes: Bundled Budda Superdrive 45 Series II style amp (parody brand 'Ganddi'), full front panel (Master[pull Channel], Bass, Mid[pull Modern], Treble, Drive, Rhythm[pull Brite], Input/Footswitch) modelled component-by-component from the local Superdrive45 manual + schematic. Two channels (Rhythm clean + Hi-gain lead off the cascaded Drive); 12AX7 cascade -> Budda tone stack -> 12AX7 LTP PI -> 5AR4/GZ34-style B+ nodes -> 2xKT66 power amp, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain knob drives the Drive (hi-gain distortion); Bass/Mid/Treble->Budda tone stack (Channel pinned Hi-gain + Modern ON via _static).

Amp_HG500.name: Lovolt DR504
Amp_HG500.notes: Bundled Hiwatt DR504 'Custom Hiwatt 50' style amp (parody brand 'Lovolt'), full front panel (Normal Vol, Brilliant Vol, Bass, Treble, Middle, Presence, Master Vol, 4 jumperable inputs) modelled component-by-component from the local DR504 schematic. A high-headroom clean-and-loud EL34 amp (3xECC83 + ECC81-style low-gain LTP PI + stiff diode B+ nodes + 2xEL34 ~50W) with two jumperable channels + a shared tone stack (strong Hiwatt mids, 100K mid pot) + Master Volume. the game's Gain knob drives the Brilliant Vol (the breakup driver); Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence (input pinned BOTH/jumpered + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_HG100.name: Lovolt DR103
Amp_HG100.notes: Bundled Hiwatt DR103 'Custom Hiwatt 100' style amp (parody brand 'Lovolt'), the 100W sibling of the DR504. Same panel (Normal Vol, Brilliant Vol, Bass, Treble, Middle, Presence, Master Vol, 4 jumperable inputs) + same preamp/tone stack, but with ECC81-style low-gain LTP PI, very stiff diode B+ nodes and 4xEL34 (~100W): even more clean headroom, less sag, bigger/tighter lows (the big clean-and-loud DR103, incl. the bridged jumpered voice). the game's Gain drives the Brilliant Vol; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence (input pinned BOTH + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_HG180.name: Marsten VS100
Amp_HG180.notes: Bundled Marshall Valvestate VS100RH style HYBRID head (parody brand 'Marsten'), full front panel (Clean Volume/Bass/Middle/Treble, Overdrive 1 Gain/Volume, Overdrive 2 Gain/Contour/Volume/Bass/Middle/Treble, FX Mix, Clean Reverb, OD Reverb, 3-mode channel) modelled component-by-component from the local v100-6x schematics. Solid-state op-amp preamp + real diode OD2 clipper + starved 12AX7 warmth stage into a solid-state power amp: CLEAN, OD1 crunch, and the OD2 diode-clip high-gain lead. the game's Gain drives OD2 Gain (the lead voice); Bass/Mid/Treble->OD2 stack (Channel pinned OD2 + reverb/FX off + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_BT30.name: Unparallel DC30
Amp_BT30.notes: Bundled Matchless DC30 style boutique head (parody brand 'Unparallel'), full front panel (Channel 1 Brilliant: Volume/Bass/Treble; Channel 2 EF86: Volume/Tone; shared Cut/Master) modelled component-by-component from the local DC30 schematic. Top Boost/EF86 channels -> 12AX7 LTP PI with Cut -> GZ34-style B+ nodes -> 4xEL84 class-A power, no global NFB, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives Channel 1 Volume; Bass/Treble->Ch1 top-boost stack.

Amp_BT15.name: Unparallel Chieftain
Amp_BT15.notes: Bundled Matchless Chieftain (Reverb) style boutique head (parody brand 'Unparallel'), full front panel (Volume, Bass, Middle, Treble, Brilliance, Master, Reverb) modelled component-by-component from the local Chieftain schematic. 12AX7 preamp -> Matchless TMB -> LTP PI/Brilliance -> GZ34-style B+ nodes -> 2xEL34 cathode-biased class AB (~40W), with spring reverb and host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives Volume; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack.

Amp_GB38.name: Mr. Y MAZ 38
Amp_GB38.notes: Bundled Dr. Z Maz 38 (Senior NR) style head (parody brand 'Mr. Y'). Amp_GB38 is the Maz 38; no Maz 38 schematic was found, but the Maz line shares one preamp + tone stack traced from the Maz 18 Jr schematic and the 38 has a larger/tighter EL84 power section. 12AX7 preamp -> Vox-meets-Fender TMB -> LTP PI/Cut -> stiff B+ nodes -> EL84 power, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives Volume; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack.

Amp_GB50.name: Mr. Y EMS
Amp_GB50.notes: Bundled Dr. Z EMS style head (parody brand 'Mr. Y'), a Marshall JCM800/JTM50-style master-volume amp: full panel (Presence, Bass, Middle, Treble, Volume[master], Gain[preamp] + HI/LO gain switch) modelled from the EMS reference (a JCM800 2204 + HI/LO). Cascaded 12AX7 preamp -> Marshall TMB tone stack -> master -> ECC83 LTP PI -> diode B+ nodes -> 2xEL34 (~50W) + presence NFB, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim; HI = full JCM800 gain, LO = JTM50 levels. the game's Gain drives the GAIN (preamp drive); Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence (Master + HI via _static).

Amp_TW22.name: TW22
Amp_TW22.notes: Bundled Fender Super-Sonic 22 / 6V6 style amp based on the local TW22 schematic, using the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, and Treble knobs. Includes bypassable fallback Cab Sim for auditioning without an external cabinet/IR.

Amp_TW26.name: TW26
Amp_TW26.notes: Bundled Fender 57 Deluxe / 5E3 tweed style amp based on the local TW26 schematic, using the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, Treble, and Pres knobs. Includes bypassable fallback Cab Sim for auditioning without an external cabinet/IR.

Amp_TW40.name: Bender Bassman
Amp_TW40.notes: Bundled Fender Bassman 5F6-A tweed style amp (parody brand 'Bender'), full front panel (2 jumperable channels Bright/Normal with their own Volume, real FMV tone stack Treble/Bass/Middle, Presence, clickable input cable) modelled component-by-component from the local 5F6-A schematic. The 5F6-A has no gain knob, so the game's Gain drives the Bright Volume; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence. Includes bypassable fallback Cab Sim for auditioning without an external cabinet/IR.

Bass_Amp_BT880B.name: FreddyKrueger800BR
Bass_Amp_BT880B.notes: Bundled Gallien-Krueger 800RB style bass head (the game 'BT880B' = GK RB800) modeled from the 800RB service manual, using the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, and Treble knobs.

Amp_AT20.name: SamplegSBTCL
Amp_AT20.notes: the game's Amp_AT20 is the Ampeg SVT-CL (the gear map's generic name is 'Marshall JCM 1'). It reuses the SAME bundled amp as Bass_Amp_BT975B — the Sampleg SBT-CL (Ampeg SVT-CL parody) — so AT-20 songs play the SVT-CL model. RS Gain/Bass/Mid/Treble map exactly as the BT975B.

Amp_AT120.name: Sampleg VH-140C
Amp_AT120.notes: Bundled Ampeg VH-140C style amp (parody brand 'Sampleg'), full front panel (Chorus Rate/Depth A/B, Reverb A/B, Channel B Level/High/Mid/Low/Gain, Channel A Level/High/Ultra-Mid/Low/Gain, channel select) modelled component-by-component from the local VH-140C service schematic. A SOLID-STATE 2x70W head (op-amps + 1N914 diode clipping, TDA power): Channel A clean + Channel B the famous tight diode-clipped lead/metal distortion, with bypassable fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives Channel B Gain; Bass/Mid/Treble->Channel B Low/Mid/High (Channel pinned B + reverb/chorus OFF via _static).

Amp_OrangeOR100.name: Citrus OR100
Amp_OrangeOR100.notes: Bundled Orange OR100 (vintage 'Graphic' head) style amp (parody brand 'Citrus'), full panel (Gain[HF Drive], Bass, Middle[FAC], Treble, Depth, Volume + FULL/HALF) modelled component-by-component from the complete local OR100 hand-drawn schematic. Single-channel British EL34 (~100W): ECC83 preamp -> Orange passive tone/Depth -> low-drive LTP PI -> stiff diode B+ nodes -> 4xEL34 power amp, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives the Gain; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack (Volume + Depth pinned via _static).

Amp_OrangeOR50H.name: Citrus OR50
Amp_OrangeOR50H.notes: Bundled Orange OR50H style amp (parody brand 'Citrus'), the 50W sibling of the OR100. Reconstructed from the local OR-family schematics with a 2xEL34 (~50W) power amp: same panel (Gain, Bass, Middle, Treble, Depth, Volume + FULL/HALF) + preamp/tone stack, but less headroom, earlier breakup, GZ34-style B+ sag and tighter lows. ECC83 preamp -> Orange tone/Depth -> low-drive LTP PI -> 2xEL34, with host-controlled fallback Cab Sim. the game's Gain drives the Gain; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack (Volume + Depth pinned via _static).

Bass_Amp_BT975B.name: SamplegSBTCL
Bass_Amp_BT975B.notes: Bundled Ampeg SVT-CL style all-tube bass head (the game 'BT975B'); preamp modeled from the documented SVT-CL circuit, using the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, and Treble knobs.

Bass_Amp_CH300B.name: SharkeHB3500
Bass_Amp_CH300B.notes: Bundled Hartke HA3500 style bass head (assigned to the game 'CH300B'/'CLH-300B', which exposes Gain/Bass/Mid/Treble + Mid-Freq/Enhance): tube+solid-state preamp blend, compressor, 10-band graphic EQ, low/high-pass, master. Modeled from the HA3500 circuit diagram. RS Gain->Solid State; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->graphic-EQ 125/500/4k bands. (CS300B freed for a Fender Rumble.)

Bass_Amp_CH600B.name: SharkeHB5000
Bass_Amp_CH600B.notes: Bundled Hartke HA5000 style bass head (the game 'CH600B'): 2x250W bi-amp, tube+solid-state preamp blend, compressor, 10-band graphic EQ, contour low/high-pass, master. Modeled from the HA5000 circuit diagram (board 4005182801). RS Gain->Solid State; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->graphic-EQ 125/500/5k bands.

Bass_Amp_BT600B.name: PeeBeeTMinus
Bass_Amp_BT600B.notes: Bundled Peavey T-Max style two-channel bass head (the game 'BT600B'): component-level model from the T-Max preamp schematic (Peavey drawing 70985). 12AX7 tube channel (Tube Pre/Post) + solid-state channel (rail-clipping CLIP), Channel Select/Combine, Shelving Low/High, 7-band graphic EQ (40/100/250/625/1.6k/4k/10k), biamp Balance/X-Over, Master. RS Gain->Tube Pre; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->graphic-EQ 100/625/4k bands.

Bass_Amp_CS300B.name: BenderFumble800
Bass_Amp_CS300B.notes: Bundled Fender Rumble Bass (1995) style all-tube dual-channel bass head (the game 'CS300B'): circuit-real model from the service schematics (drawings 048406 preamp / 048411 power) — two 12AX7 preamp channels (A vintage / B), each Volume + passive Fender TMB (Treble/Bass/Middle) + Mid-cut switch, blended by MIX, into a 12AT7 LTP phase inverter + 6x6550 push-pull power amp. RS Gain->B Volume; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->B Bass/Middle/Treble. (Display: Bender Fumble Bass.)

Bass_Amp_EdenE300.name: AidenGT300
Bass_Amp_EdenE300.notes: Bundled Eden WT-300 'Traveler' Valve-Tech style hybrid bass preamp (the game 'EdenE300'): component-level model from the Eden WT-300 preamp schematic (2-19-93) — 12AX7 input, Eden opto Comp, Enhance contour, Bass/Treble shelves, 3-band semi-parametric EQ (30-300/200-2k/1.2-12k), Master. RS Gain->Gain; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->Bass/EQ2 mid/Treble.

Bass_Amp_EdenWT550.name: AidenGT550
Bass_Amp_EdenWT550.notes: Bundled Eden WT-550 'Traveler 550' Valve-Tech style hybrid bass preamp (the game 'EdenWT550'): same Valve-Tech Twin-Triode preamp as the WT-300 in the 550 W head, modeled from the WT-550 preamp schematic (WT550PreEQ). 12AX7 input, Eden Comp, Enhance, Bass/Treble shelves, 3-band semi-parametric EQ (Low/Mid/High), Master. RS Gain->Gain; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->Bass/EQ2 mid/Treble.

Bass_Amp_EdenWT800.name: AidenGT880
Bass_Amp_EdenWT800.notes: Bundled Eden WT-880 'World Tour' Valve-Tech style hybrid bass head (the game 'EdenWt800'): component-level model from the Eden WT-800C schematic. Same Eden preamp as the WT-300/550 (12AX7 input, Comp, Enhance, Bass/Treble shelves, 3-band semi-parametric Low/Mid/High EQ) plus the WT-880 bi-amp (Crossover Freq + Balance + X-Over on/off), Master. RS Gain->Gain; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->Bass/Mid Level/Treble.

Bass_Amp_HT100B.name: Lovolt100
Bass_Amp_HT100B.notes: Bundled Custom Hiwatt 100 (DR103) style all-tube head (the game 'HT100B'): component-level model from the documented DR103 circuit + factory spec — 12AX7 Normal/Bright preamp, British (FMV) Bass/Middle/Treble tone stack, Presence (NFB), 4x EL34 push-pull (~100 W, big clean Hiwatt headroom). RS Gain->Normal Vol; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack.

Bass_Amp_HT400B.name: SillaBoogieBass400
Bass_Amp_HT400B.notes: Bundled Mesa/Boogie Bass 400+ style all-tube head (the game 'HT400B'): component-level model from the Bass 400+ panel + circuit — 12AX7 preamp (Volume 1/2, pull-Bright), passive Middle/Bass/Treble (pull-Shift) tone stack, Mesa 6-band graphic EQ (40/100/250/625/1560/3900), 12x 6L6 push-pull (~500 W, huge clean tube headroom). RS Gain->Volume 1; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack.

Bass_Amp_OrangeAD200B.name: CitrusAD200
Bass_Amp_OrangeAD200B.notes: Bundled Orange AD200B (MK3) style all-tube bass head (the game 'OrangeAD200B'): component-level model from the AD200B panel + manual (tone-stack character cross-checked vs the BAJA AD200B emulator). 12AX7 input (Gain), Orange PASSIVE SUBTRACTIVE tone stack (Bass/Middle/Treble flat at full up, cut below), 4x KT88 push-pull (~200 W, big clean headroom). RS Gain->Gain; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack (offset so RS-flat=flat, lower cuts).

Bass_Amp_CS240B.name: TracerV8
Bass_Amp_CS240B.notes: Bundled Trace Elliot V-Type V8 style 400 W all-valve bass head (the game 'CS240B'): component-level model from the V-Type V8 schematic (cd0119/cd0120). All-ECC83 preamp (Gain I+Bright, Gain II+Pull, Level), Trace tone stack (Bass+Deep, Middle+Shift, Treble), opto compressor, 8x KT88 push-pull (~400 W). RS Gain->Gain I; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack.

Bass_Amp_HT300B.name: SbrRedhead
Bass_Amp_HT300B.notes: Bundled SWR Super Redhead style hybrid bass head (the game 'HT300B'): component-level model from the SWR Red Head preamp schematic. 12AX7 preamp (Gain+Turbo), SWR Aural Enhancer contour, Bass shelf, semi-parametric Mid (Level+Freq), Treble shelf (+Transparency), 350 W solid-state power (clean hi-fi). RS Gain->Gain; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->Bass/Mid Level/Treble.

Bass_Amp_LT25B.name: DustupCDN
Bass_Amp_LT25B.notes: Bundled Ashdown ABM EVO (Bass Magnifier) style head (the game 'LT25B'): component-level model from the Ashdown ABM500 EVO preamp schematic. ECC83 Valve Drive, Bass/Middle/Treble, 6-band graphic EQ (100/180/340/1.3k/3.6k/5k), Sub-octave generator, opto Comp, Shape, Output. RS Gain->Input; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->Bass/Middle/Treble.

Bass_Amp_LT85B.name: ElectricB600F
Bass_Amp_LT85B.notes: Bundled Acoustic B600H style 600 W solid-state bass head (the game 'LT85B'): component-level model from the Acoustic B450/B600H schematic. Gain/Volume preamp, sweepable Notch (Freq+On), 6-band EQ (40/120/350/800/2k/5k), Mute, Class-D power (clean). RS Gain->Gain; RS Bass/Mid/Treble->EQ 120/800/5k bands.

Bass_Amp_CS75B.name: SamplegV4B
Bass_Amp_CS75B.notes: Bundled Ampeg V-4B style all-tube bass head (the game 'CS75B'); component-level model from the 1971 V-4B schematic (12AX7 preamp, 4x 7027A ~100W), using the game's Gain, Bass, Mid, and Treble knobs.

Bass_Amp_CH350B.name: MarstenDBS7400
Bass_Amp_CH350B.notes: Bundled Marshall DBS 7400 (Dynamic Bass System) style solid-state bass head (the game 'CS350B'/'CLH-350B'); component-level model from the 7400 schematic (op-amp preamp + Lo/Hi Primary EQ + Depth-only compressor + the real 9-band gyrator graphic EQ 50/80/160/320/640/1.25k/2.5k/5k/8k). Uses RS Gain, Bass->Lo, Treble->Hi; RS's 7 graphic bands are remapped to the nearest of the 9.

Amp_OrangeTinyTerror.name: CitrusBigTremor
Amp_OrangeTinyTerror.notes: Bundled Orange Tiny Terror style single-channel guitar head (the game 'OrangeTinyTerror'): component-level model from the Tiny Terror schematic. Two 12AX7 gain stages, single passive Tone, Volume, real 12AX7 LTP PI, B+ sag nodes, 2x EL84 cathode-biased push-pull (~15 W; 7 W Half mode) and bypassable fallback cab sim. RS Gain->Gain; RS Tone->Tone.

Amp_OrangeRockerverb.name: CitrusRumbleverb50
Amp_OrangeRockerverb.notes: Bundled Orange Rockerverb 50 MkII style 2-channel guitar head (the game 'OrangeRockerverb'): component-level model from the Rockerverb 50 schematic. Clean (Vol/Bass/Treble) + Dirty (Gain/Bass/Middle/Treble/Vol) channels, valve spring Reverb, Output master, real 12AX7 LTP PI, B+ sag nodes, 2x EL34 push-pull and bypassable fallback cab sim. RS Gain/Bass/Mid/Treble drive the Dirty channel (default channel).

Amp_EpiphoneElectarCentury.name: EpicallCentura
Amp_EpiphoneElectarCentury.notes: Bundled Epiphone Electar Century (75th reissue) style 15W guitar head (the game 'EpiphoneElectarCentury'): component-level model from the Century 75th-reissue schematic. Two 12AX7 stages, passive Tone, input Voice (Dark/Normal/Bright), pull-Boost, cathodyne PI, B+ sag nodes, 2x 6V6 push-pull and bypassable fallback cab sim. RS Volume(Gain)->Volume.

Amp_EpiphoneElectarM.name: EpicallCentura
Amp_EpiphoneElectarM.notes: Bundled alias for the game 'EpiphoneElectarM'. Local curation maps this gear to the same Epiphone Valve Jr / Electar Century family capture as Amp_EpiphoneElectarCentury, so it uses EpicallCentura with the same component-level DSP and knob mapping.

Amp_EpiphoneElectricHawaiian.name: EpicallCentura
Amp_EpiphoneElectricHawaiian.notes: Bundled alias for the game 'EpiphoneElectricHawaiian'. Local curation maps this gear to the same Epiphone Valve Jr / Electar Century family capture as Amp_EpiphoneElectarCentury, so it uses EpicallCentura with the same component-level DSP and knob mapping.

Amp_EpiphoneZephyr.name: EpicallRuby
Amp_EpiphoneZephyr.notes: Bundled Epiphone Electar Zephyr Amp20 (~1949) style vintage guitar amp (the game 'EpiphoneZephyr'): component-level model from the 1949 Zephyr schematic. 12AX7 preamp, passive Bass/Treble, LTP PI approximation, 5U4-style B+ sag nodes, 2x 6L6G/6L6 push-pull (~20W) and bypassable fallback cab sim. RS Volume(Gain)/Bass/Treble.

Amp_GibsonGA8.name: HipzonGA8
Amp_GibsonGA8.notes: Bundled Gibson GA-8 Discoverer (GA-8T) style guitar amp (the game 'GibsonGA8'): component-level from the GA-8T schematic. 12AX7 preamp, passive Bass/Treble, cathodyne PI, 5Y3-style B+ sag nodes, EL84-family fallback model for 2x 6BM8 push-pull (~10W), on-board tremolo (Speed/Depth), and bypassable cab sim. RS Volume(Loudness)/Bass/Treble.

Amp_GibsonGA79.name: HipzonGA79RVT
Amp_GibsonGA79.notes: Bundled Gibson GA-79 RVT Multi Stereo style guitar amp (the game 'GibsonGA79'): component-level from the GA-79 RVT schematic. 12AX7 preamp, passive Bass/Treble, valve spring Reverb, low-gain LTP PI approximation, B+ sag nodes, 4x 6BQ5 (EL84-family) push-pull, tremolo (Speed/Depth), and bypassable cab sim; modeled mono. RS Volume/Bass/Treble.

Amp_GibsonGA88.name: HipzonGA88
Amp_GibsonGA88.notes: Bundled Gibson GA-88S stereo style guitar amp (the game 'GibsonGA88'): component-level from the GA-88S schematic. 12AX7 preamp, passive Bass/Treble, low-gain LTP PI approximation, GZ34-style B+ nodes, 4x 6BQ5 (EL84-family) push-pull (~30W, clean PA headroom), and bypassable cab sim; modeled mono. RS Volume/Bass/Treble.

Amp_MarshallJCM800.name: Marsten JCM800
Amp_MarshallJCM800.notes: Bundled Marshall JCM800 2204 (50W master-volume) style head (parody brand 'Marsten'), full front panel (Presence, Bass, Middle, Treble, Master Vol, Preamp Vol + High/Low inputs) modelled component-by-component from the official Marshall 2204 STD schematic (2204prem/2204pwrm). Cascaded 12AX7 preamp (Preamp Vol = the drive) -> Marshall TMB tone stack (Treble 220k, Bass 1M, Middle 22k, slope 33k, 470pF/22n) -> master -> 12AX7 long-tail PI -> 2xEL34 (~50W) + Presence NFB. the game's Gain drives the Preamp Vol; Bass/Mid/Treble->tone stack, Pres->Presence (Master + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_MarshallJTM45.name: Marsten JTM45
Amp_MarshallJTM45.notes: Bundled Marshall JTM45 style head (parody 'Marsten'), the Plexi predecessor: Presence/Bass/Middle/Treble + Loudness 1 (High Treble) + Loudness 2 (Normal) + jumperable inputs, modelled from the local JTM45 schematic. 2x5881/6L6 (~30W) + GZ34 tube rectifier -> warm, sag, early breakup. NON-master: RS Gain -> Loudness 1; Bass/Mid/Treble->stack, Pres->Presence (Loudness 2 + Input + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_Marshall1962Bluesbreaker.name: Marsten Bluesbreaker
Amp_Marshall1962Bluesbreaker.notes: Bundled Marshall 1962 Bluesbreaker style combo (parody 'Marsten'): a JTM45 voice (Presence/Bass/Middle/Treble + Loudness 1/2 + jumper inputs, 2x5881/6L6 ~30W + GZ34) PLUS a power-amp Tremolo (Speed/Intensity). RS Gain -> Loudness 1; Bass/Mid/Treble->stack, Pres->Presence (Loudness 2 + Input + tremolo off + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_MarshallDSL15H.name: Marsten DSL15
Amp_MarshallDSL15H.notes: Bundled Marshall DSL15H style head (parody 'Marsten'): 2 channels (Classic Gain clean/crunch + Ultra Gain OD), shared EQ (Presence/Bass/Middle/Treble) + Deep + Tone Shift, ~15W. Component-level ECC83 cascade into LTP PI, diode B+ nodes and 2x6V6 power amp. RS Gain -> Ultra Gain (channel pinned Ultra); Bass/Mid/Treble->EQ, Pres->Presence (volumes + Deep/Tone Shift + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_MarshallJVM410H.name: Marsten JVM410
Amp_MarshallJVM410H.notes: Bundled Marshall JVM410H style head (parody 'Marsten'): a 4-channel (Clean/Crunch/OD1/OD2, each green/orange/red) 100W cascading Marshall + Presence/Resonance/Master/Reverb. Modelled as the selected channel+mode (one sound per song), with ECC83 LTP PI, stiff diode B+ nodes and 4xEL34 power amp. RS Gain -> Gain (Channel pinned OD1, Mode orange via _static); Bass/Mid/Treble->stack, Pres->Presence (Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_EN50.name: Engel Fireball
Amp_EN50.notes: Bundled ENGL Fireball 100 style head (parody 'Engel'): 2-channel high-gain metal (Clean Gain + Lead Gain, shared Bass/Middle/Treble, Lead Volume, Master, Presence + Bright/Bottom/Mid-Boost switches), ~100W 6L6GC, from the Fireball schematic. Adds real ECC83 LTP PI, stiff silicon B+ nodes and bypassable fallback cab sim. RS Gain -> Lead Gain (channel pinned Lead); Bass/Mid/Treble->stack, Pres->Presence.

Amp_CS100.name: Polystone MiniBrute
Amp_CS100.notes: Bundled Polytone Mini-Brute style solid-state jazz combo (parody 'Polystone'): a warm, clean jazz-box voice (Volume, Bass, Treble + Brite switch, no middle). Op-amp clean preamp + Baxandall tone, stays clean, with bypassable fallback Cab Sim. RS Gain -> Volume; Bass/Treble->tone (no Mid on this amp).

Amp_CS120.name: Ronald JC-120
Amp_CS120.notes: Bundled Roland JC-120 Jazz Chorus style amp (parody 'Ronald'), the 2x12 stereo sibling of the Ronald JC-90: solid-state clean voice + Distortion (clean at 0) + stereo BBD Chorus + spring Reverb, with bypassable fallback Cab Sim. RS Gain -> Distortion (clean at 0); Bass/Mid/Treble->stack (Volume/Reverb/Chorus via _static).

Amp_OrangeAD50.name: Citrus AD50
Amp_OrangeAD50.notes: Bundled Orange AD50 Custom Shop style single-channel head (parody brand 'Citrus'), the game 'OrangeAD50'. No AD50 schematic exists; reconstructed in the OR/OR120 lineage (the user confirmed the online AD30 schematic is a different 2-channel EL84 circuit and does not apply). Extra-hot 12AX7 gain stage, 2-band shelving EQ (Bass/Treble), real 12AX7 LTP PI, B+ sag nodes, EL34 power amp, power-amp NFB Presence, Master, Sustain (EQ-bypass boost) + Class A (30W, earlier breakup) / AB (50W), and bypassable fallback cab sim. RS Gain->Gain; Bass/Treble->EQ; Pres->Presence (Master + Sustain + Class A + Cab Sim via _static).

Amp_OrangeJimmyBean.name: Citrus Jimmy Bean
Amp_OrangeJimmyBean.notes: Bundled Orange Jimmy Bean (JB150, 1975) style ~150W SOLID-STATE twin-channel combo (parody brand 'Citrus'), the game 'OrangeJimmyBean'. No schematic exists; reconstructed from web research (denim/leather op-amp era twin-channel with built-in tremolo + sustain). Op-amp clean stage, Baxandall Bass/Treble, Bright shelf, Channel voicing, Sustain (compressor/soft-clip), tremolo (Speed 2-8Hz / Depth, 0=off), and bypassable fallback Cab Sim. RS Gain->Sustain; Bass/Treble->EQ (Volume + Speed/Depth/Channel/Bright via _static).

DI_Amp_TubePre.name: Multiversal 610
DI_Amp_TubePre.notes: Bundled Universal Audio 610-A console tube DI/preamp (parody brand 'Multiversal'), the game 'DI_Amp_TubePre'/'Amp Tube Pre'. Component-level model from the local 610-A schematic (John Hinson redraw): UTC O-1 input transformer -> 12AX7 cascade -> Lo/Hi gain -> passive stepped LF/HF shelf EQ -> 12AY7 -> UTC PA-5946 output transformer. A DI (no cab). RS Gain->Gain; Bass->Low EQ; Treble->High EQ (the real 610 has no mid, so RS Mid/MidFreq are dropped). Level/Hi Gain/Mic pinned via _static.

DI_Amp_BassDriver.name: SinAmp Bass Driver
DI_Amp_BassDriver.notes: Bundled Tech 21 SansAmp Bass Driver DI (V2) style analog bass preamp (parody brand 'SinAmp'), the game 'DI_Amp_BassDriver'/'Amp Bass Driver'. Component-level model from the local V2 schematic: TLC2272 op-amp chain, PRESENCE pre-clip HF boost, DRIVE into 3.3V zener clippers, fixed post-clip speaker-emulation filters, BLEND (dry vs amp), BASS/MID/TREBLE active tone (+ shifts), LEVEL. The speaker voicing is part of the box. RS Gain->Drive; Bass->Bass; Treble->Treble; Blend->Blend; Pres->Presence (Mid/Level/shifts via _static).

DI_Amp_MixerPre.name: Meve 1073
DI_Amp_MixerPre.notes: Bundled Neve 1073 channel amplifier (class-A console preamp/EQ used as a DI; parody brand 'Meve'), the game 'DI_Amp_MixerPre'/'Amp Mixer Pre'. Component-level model from the local 1073 schematics (EH10023 + BA283/BA284 class-A cards + B182/C HPF + B205 hi/lo EQ + B211 presence + LO1166 output trafo): Marinair-ish input iron, progressive class-A 'Neve push', LOW shelf, PRESENCE mid peak, HIGH shelf, 3rd-order HPF. A DI (no cab). RS Gain->Gain; Bass->Low; Mid->Mid; Treble->High (Low/Mid freq + HPF + Output via _static).

Bass_Pedal_NYRBS103.name: NYRBS103
Bass_Pedal_NYRBS103.notes: Bundled original monophonic bass synth (MVP): pitch-tracking oscillator + square sub-octave through a resonant 4-pole low-pass with a per-note filter envelope. Maps Mix/Sub/Cutoff/Resonance/Envelope/Shape/Level.
